title: Milk Truck Mug Delivery Run
description: A CesiumMilkTruck drives along a physics-enabled road delivering milk into coffee mugs placed at stations. The user controls the truck speed and must stop precisely at each mug station, then tilt the truck to pour milk. Overshoot or pour too fast and the mug tips over due to simulated force.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a milk truck delivery physics simulation. Load CesiumMilkTruck.glb and five instances of coffeeMug.glb using GLTFLoader. Set up a ground plane (80x10 units, gray material) as the road. Place the truck at the left end and space the five mugs evenly along the road on small platform pedestals. Use a perspective camera at position (0, 8, 15) looking at the road center, with OrbitControls for viewing. Add a directional light at (5, 10, 5) intensity 1.5 and ambient light intensity 0.4. Implement a simple physics system: the truck moves forward along the X-axis with velocity controlled by an on-screen slider (range 0-5 units/sec). Each mug has a tipping threshold: if the truck stops within 0.5 units of a mug station, a Pour button appears. Clicking Pour triggers a tilt animation on the truck (rotate Z by 15 degrees over 1 second). During pouring, a white cylinder particle stream appears from the truck to the mug. Track a fill level per mug (0-100%) shown as a rising white cylinder inside the mug. The user must release the Pour button before fill exceeds 100%, otherwise the mug tips over (rotate X by 90 degrees with a 1-second ease-out). If the truck overshoots a station (passes beyond 0.5 units), that mug is marked as missed. Display an HUD overlay showing: current speed, mugs filled (0/5), mugs tipped, mugs missed. The user must successfully fill all 5 mugs without tipping or missing to win. Add parameter validation: speed must be non-negative, fill level clamped 0-100, truck position bounded to road length. Show a success or failure message after the last station. The interaction steps are: adjust speed, stop at station 1, pour into mug 1, repeat for stations 2-5, and receive final score.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  truckPositionX: number,         // current X position of the milk truck along the road
  truckVelocity: number,         // current velocity of truck in units per second (0-5)
  currentStationIndex: number,         // index of the mug station the truck is approaching (0-4)
  isPouringActive: boolean,         // true when Pour button is pressed and milk is flowing
  truckTiltAngle: number,         // current Z-axis rotation angle of truck in degrees during pour
  activeMugFillLevel: number,         // fill percentage (0-100) of the mug currently being filled
  tippedMugCount: number,         // count of mugs that have tipped over due to overfill
  successfulDeliveries: number,         // count of mugs successfully filled without tipping
  distanceToNextStation: number,         // distance in units from truck to next mug station
  totalMugStations: number,         // total number of mug stations on the road (always 5)
  isStoppedAtStation: boolean,         // true when truck is within stopping range of a station
  gamePhase: string,         // current phase - driving|pouring|completed
}
